jurisdiction_name: Canada - Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA)
description: Canadian privacy law protecting personal information in the private sector

child.description: Parental consent required for children under 16

young_adult.description: Parent should be notified and can withdraw consent

principles: Identifying purposes - clearly state why data is collected
Consent - obtain valid consent before collection
Limiting collection - only collect necessary data
Limiting use - use only for stated purpose
Access - allow individuals to access their data
Complaint handling - respond to privacy complaints

consent_documentation.description: Document consent from parents and students

data_access_process.description: Must respond to data access requests within 30 days

data_correction_process.description: Allow individuals to request correction of inaccurate data

warnings: PIPEDA applies to private sector organizations in Canada
Parental consent required for children under 16
Must designate someone responsible for privacy compliance
Cannot use location data without explicit consent
requirements: Identify purposes before collecting personal information
Obtain explicit parental consent for children under 16
Only collect data necessary for identified purposes
Protect data with appropriate security measures
Allow access to and correction of data
